title: V4 Communication Upgrade Review
objective: Explain why ProductOS should move from readable docs to a governed communication and presentation system for stakeholder alignment.
decision_required: Approve the V4.1 communication and presentation packaging slice.
delivery_context: Leadership review after the V4 readable-doc slice was validated.
narrative_goal: Lead with the decision, show benchmarked communication gains, and keep remaining governance gaps visible.
success_outcome: Leadership should approve the next bounded communication slice and understand why it is a governance upgrade rather than cosmetic polish.
audience_constraints: Keep the recommendation readable without live narration.
Keep remaining metadata and pattern-governance gaps visible.
required_objections: Why are readable docs alone not enough for leadership alignment?
How do we stop stronger presentation quality from overstating certainty?
non_negotiables: Do not let slides become the source of truth.
Do not hide unresolved governance gaps behind cleaner presentation.
comparison_baseline: V4 readable-doc bundle without presentation packaging

slide_outlines.title: Why V4 Needs Presentation Packaging
slide_outlines.audience_question: What decision should leadership make now that the readable-doc layer exists?
slide_outlines.claim: ProductOS should approve a governed presentation layer so readable docs can become leadership-ready communication without manual PM rewriting.
proof_requirements: Show that the readable-doc system is real but not yet enough for executive alignment
slide_outlines.core_message: Readable docs solved traceability, but leadership alignment still needs a governed narrative and rendering layer.
key_points: Executive-safe packaging is the next gap
slide_outlines.speaker_notes: Open with the decision and make clear that this is a communication-system upgrade, not a styling request.

slide_outlines.title: What Improved And What Did Not
slide_outlines.audience_question: What changed after the readable-doc slice, and where is the remaining stakeholder-alignment gap?
slide_outlines.claim: The readable-doc bundle reduced raw JSON dependence, but alignment still slows down when leadership-ready packaging is missing.
proof_requirements: Show the current doc bundle is usable
Show the outcome review still calls for another iteration
slide_outlines.core_message: The system is now reviewable, but not yet fully optimized for stakeholder alignment speed.
key_points: Manual validation accepted the doc bundle
slide_outlines.cta: Use presentation packaging to shorten stakeholder review cycles.
slide_outlines.speaker_notes: Show both the gain and the remaining gap so the recommendation stays grounded.

slide_outlines.title: What Keeps This Safe
slide_outlines.audience_question: How do we prevent a stronger presentation system from turning into ungoverned persuasion?
slide_outlines.claim: Presentation packaging is acceptable only if audience policy, pattern review, publish checks, and manual validation stay explicit.
proof_requirements: Show which gaps remain blocked until pattern governance is in place
slide_outlines.core_message: Narrative power must stay subordinate to evidence, validation, and manual approval.
key_points: Publishing remains blocked without explicit controls
slide_outlines.cta: Treat pattern review and publish checks as part of the implementation, not post-work.
slide_outlines.speaker_notes: Close by showing that the proposal is disciplined and supervised.
slide_outlines.visual_direction: Risk matrix with visible control labels.

facts.statement: The workspace now has a readable discovery, strategy, prototype, roadmap, and PRD document bundle.
The doc bundle is usable but still carries targeted review and publishing-metadata follow-up work.
Outcome review says the readable-doc bundle improved internal reviewability but still needs another iteration for external-ready communication.
Internal adoption was allowed only because the unresolved publishing and pattern-governance questions stayed explicit.
